trajectory.description: Observation: Starting fresh at iteration 0 with no prior workspace, training curves, or eval scores. Analysis: A working end-to-end pipeline is required immediately per workflow (few epochs then submit_eval to baseline score + measure iteration time). Using a simple but functional FCN on pretrained ResNet-50 (25M params, listed as pre-downloaded) avoids loading issues; 512x512 crops match meta.json; remap labels per spec (0->255, 1-150->0-149); log metrics to required JSONL; predict.py batches at fixed resize for speed/original-size output with nearest-neighbor. This gives a quick baseline mIoU (expected low ~15-25 after 2 epochs) without OOM or network dependency. Decision: Write model/train/predict scripts, train 2 epochs, submit best checkpoint for eval to get first score and time data before iterating on architecture/augs/lr/epochs.

trajectory.description: Observation: Train failed immediately on albumentations init (RandomResizedCrop scale>1.0 invalid in this version + size expects tuple/kwargs; val/predict Resize likely affected too). No training or eval occurred. Analysis: Pydantic validation in current albumentations version enforces scale <=1.0 (area ratio) and explicit height/width; simple FCN-ResNet50 baseline otherwise valid (pre-downloaded weights, label remapping correct, metric/logging/predict contract followed, DataParallel missing for 4-GPU use). Decision: Fix transforms with height/width kwargs + scale=(0.5,1.0), add nn.DataParallel for all 4 GPUs, clean redundant interpolate in predict.py, set epochs=3 for quick baseline, run train then submit_eval on best.pt to obtain first mIoU and iteration timing before optimizing backbone/augs/scheduler.

Observation: albumentations init error on RandomResizedCrop (size field required in pydantic schema for this version; height/width not accepted). No training/eval in prior iters. Analysis: transform API mismatch (common in albumentations>=1.4); core pipeline (ResNet50 FCN, label remapping 0->255/1-150->0-149, DataParallel for 4 GPUs, metric logging to JSONL, predict contract with batch resize+NN resize back to orig res) is otherwise sound and matches all specs. Decision: fix only the transform param to size=(512,512), keep simple baseline, train 3 epochs, submit best.pt for first mIoU + time measurement before scaling model/backbone/augs/epochs.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training failed on CE loss with 'expected scalar type Long but found Int' (masks dtype issue). No mIoU or full timing yet. Analysis: Despite np.int64, albumentations transforms (e.g. RandomResizedCrop) can yield int32/uint8 tensors for masks; DataParallel, ResNet50 FCN, label remapping (0->255, 1-150->0-149), JSONL logging, and predict.py contract are otherwise correct and match all specs (meta.json, original-res masks, 4-GPU). Decision: Add .long() cast in dataset __getitem__ to guarantee LongTensor for loss/metric, bump batch_size to 16 + epochs=5 for better baseline while staying under time/iter budget, run train then submit_eval on best.pt for first mIoU + iteration timing before upgrading to better backbone (e.g. MIT-B5) or augs/scheduler.

trajectory.description: Observation: Achieved baseline mIoU=0.2507 (val peak 0.2466) with ResNet50-FCN after 5 epochs; loss curve shows steady decline from ~5.0 to ~1.1 indicating underfitting and room for improvement; pipeline (label remapping 0->255/1-150->0-149, DataParallel on 4 GPUs, JSONL logging, predict contract with batched 512-resize + NN resize to orig res, best.pt saving) now fully functional and stable. Analysis: Simple FCN limits performance but uses listed pretrained backbone (25M params, well under 1B limit); longer training + poly LR decay + color augmentations will boost mIoU without architecture change or OOM risk (prior run ~45min for 5 epochs). Decision: Update only train.py with epochs=15, poly scheduler, color jitter augs, num_workers=8, val_bs=32; run training then submit best.pt for eval to get improved score and confirm timing before next upgrades (e.g. DeepLabV3 head or MIT-B5 backbone).

trajectory.description: Observation: ResNet50-FCN baseline reached mIoU=0.2838 after 15 epochs (val peak 0.2799, loss down to ~0.9, still underfitting with slow gains). Pipeline is now stable (DataParallel on 4 GPUs, label remapping, JSONL logging, batched predict with resize+NN upsample, best.pt). Analysis: Simple 1x1 classifier limits performance on ADE20K; adding full ASPP (with image pooling, atrous rates, BN+ReLU+dropout) creates DeepLabV3-style head for better multi-scale context without changing backbone (stays under 1B params, uses pre-downloaded ResNet). Higher LR=5e-4 + 20 epochs + poly scheduler should yield ~0.33+ mIoU. Decision: Update model.py with ASPP+DeepLab head, adjust train.py to 20 epochs/LR=5e-4, run training then immediately submit_eval on best.pt to get new score + confirm ~2.5h timing before further upgrades (e.g. MIT-B5 or mmseg).

trajectory.error_summary: [TIMEOUT] Command exceeded 13958s limit. Consider reducing epochs or using a smaller model.
trajectory.description: Observation: mIoU improved to 0.336 (from 0.284) with ASPP/DeepLabV3-ResNet50 after 20 epochs; val mIoU peaked at 0.3376 in final epoch with loss curve still declining to ~0.7 (underfitting but stable pipeline: DataParallel 4-GPUs, label remap 0->255/1-150->0-149, JSONL logging, batched predict+NN resize). Analysis: Higher bs=24 reduces steps/epoch (~33% faster) allowing 30 epochs in ~3.1h (within 3.88h budget); bump LR to 1e-3 + extend poly scheduler to 30 epochs should yield further gains without architecture change (stays <<1B params); dynamic size= in upsample is more general. Decision: Incrementally edit model.py (dynamic upsample) + train.py (bs=24, epochs=30, lr=1e-3, scheduler update), train then submit best.pt for eval to push mIoU higher while measuring new timing.
